title: STAT investigates the epidemic killing 178,000 Americans a year
summary: Read Executive Editor Rick Berke's letter to STAT readers on "The Deadliest Drug," a new investigative series into the country's failure to address excessive alcohol use.

title: STAT+: CDC plans to transfer monkeys to nonprofit’s sanctuary as it seeks to reduce animal testing
summary: The CDC intends to transfer more than 160 macaques to Born Free USA, a nonprofit that runs a large primate sanctuary in Texas, as the agency seeks to phase out…

title: STAT+: The hunt for FDA’s next leader
summary: Marty Makary is out, and the Trump administration has a long wish list for its next FDA commissioner.

title: STAT+: MIT says research has fallen 10%, and grad student enrollment is down
summary: At MIT, research has shrunk 10% from a year ago, and the school expects a persistent drop in graduate admissions.

title: Create Medicines raises $122M to bolster in vivo CAR-T therapies
summary: The fresh funds will support the biotech's pipeline of RNA-based CAR-T cell therapies for cancer and autoimmune conditions.

title: Biogen pushing tau drug forward despite Alzheimer’s study failure
summary: One of the company’s more closely watched research assets, BIIB080 is now heading to late-stage testing based on “the strength of the biomarker and efficacy data” seen in a Phase 2 trial.

title: STAT+: BeOne wins FDA approval in lymphoma race
summary: Diamantas takes the reins, Regenxbio stakes claim in Duchenne gene therapy, and more biotech news from The Readout

title: STAT+: Why big digital health players are missing from Medicare’s chronic care experiment
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: Why big digital health players skipped Medicare's ACCESS pilot, Isomorphic's big raise, and more.

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about upbeat results for a Duchenne drug, Takeda layoffs, and much more
summary: Biogen reported mixed results from a mid-stage clinical trial investigating a treatment for Alzheimer’s disease that targets the protein tau

title: With new data, Regenxbio to seek FDA approval of Duchenne gene therapy
summary: Though two serious side effects muddied the results, CEO Curran Simpson expressed optimism about a clearance and claimed FDA leadership will have a “mandate on rare disease flexibility.”

title: STAT+: Three years of declining overdose deaths begets cautious optimism and some concerns
summary: The next FDA leader, how the CDC is responding to hantavirus, and more health news from Morning Rounds

title: STAT+: Biogen’s tau-targeting Alzheimer’s drug posts mixed results in mid-stage study
summary: Biogen is moving its tau-targeting Alzheimer's drug into Phase 3 studies, despite mixed data from a Phase 2 trial.

title: STAT+: Regenxbio says Duchenne gene therapy succeeded in clinical trial, paving way for FDA submission
summary: Regenxbio said its experimental gene therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy succeeded in a trial, paving the way for a submission to the FDA.

title: STAT+: CREATE Medicines, a biotech company developing CAR-T therapies, raises $122 million
summary: CREATE Medicines has a new name, an expanded pipeline, and now fresh funding to propel the company’s first CAR-T candidates through human trials.

title: STAT+: ‘No drama’ Diamantas should serve biotech well as acting FDA chief
summary: Kyle Diamantas, the acting commissioner of the FDA, is expected to be a drama-free, stabilizing caretaker at the top of the agency.

title: Opinion: Using AI in addiction medicine could be particularly risky
summary: "AI can make great strides in medical decision support, but unfiltered interaction between agent and patient is dangerous," writes Steve D. Klein.

title: STAT+: Treatment for alcohol addiction is undergoing a seismic shift. Many say it’s overdue
summary: The surging popularity of alternatives to Alcoholics Anonymous represents a seismic shift in the alcohol treatment landscape. Many say it's overdue.

title: Hantavirus risk remains low, CDC says, citing its ‘playbook’ response
summary: Despite rising concern about the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship, two CDC doctors say the risk to the public remains low.

title: Officials say $1.3 billion in Medicaid money to California will be deferred over suspicions of fraud
summary: Vice President JD Vance announced a $1.3 billion deferral in Medicaid reimbursements to California, as part of an initiative to root out fraud in federal health programs.

title: Opinion: The hantavirus is a wake-up call. Will the Trump administration answer it?
summary: Arriving in the isolation ward of a biocontainment hospital is an unsettling, scary experience. In 2014, I spent 19 days in one while being treated for Ebola, watching the news…

title: Opinion: Hospitals are silencing doctors online, and it’s fueling the health misinformation crisis
summary: Hospitals and health systems need to loosen the reins and let physicians combat social media misinformation, writes Adam Goodcoff.

title: STAT+: What the Trump administration wants in its next FDA leader
summary: Leaders want the next FDA commissioner to be someone who can rebuild trust, focus on food policy, and drive drug-approval reforms.

title: U.S. overdose deaths fell again in 2025, but some worry about policy and drug supply changes
summary: About 70,000 Americans died of drug overdoses last year — about 14% fewer than the previous year, according to preliminary government data.

title: FDA’s leadership void leaves biotech with renewed ‘uncertainty’
summary: Marty Makary's resignation leaves the agency with temporary directors in three top positions, and a number of newly instituted policies with an unclear future.

title: Lilly data point to ‘maintenance’ strategies for GLP-1 weight loss
summary: A pair of trial results published in medical journals suggest switching from high-dose injectables to lower doses or pills could help people with obesity keep their weight down over the long term.

title: Assertio accepts new buyout bid; AI biotech Isomorphic banks $2B
summary: Assertio’s latest offer represents a roughly 31% premium over an initial acquisition proposal. Elsewhere, Kyverna is readying a first-of-its-kind approval application and AC Immune is swapping CEOs.

title: [Podcast] Closing the Distance in Cancer Care
summary: This podcast episode explores the critical role community oncology plays in the U.S. healthcare system and why supporting its growth and autonomy matters for patients and providers.

summary: Makary’s exit ends a turbulent run marred by leadership upheaval, mass layoffs, political pressure and public spats with drugmakers.

title: Arvinas, Pfizer find new partner for ‘Protac’ breast cancer drug
summary: A deal with Rigel Pharmaceuticals ends months of uncertainty about the future of Veppanu, a first-of-its-kind, newly approved medicine with a murky financial outlook.

title: FDA Finalizes Food Chemical Safety Post-Market Assessment Program, Launches Reassessment of BHT, ADA
summary: FDA continues to implement its bold food agenda, with two decisive actions focused on food chemical safety.

title: Bristol Myers, Hengrui join forces in drugmaking alliance worth up to $15.2 billion
summary: The wide-ranging pact involves as many as 13 drugs and marks the latest example of the growing relationship between Chinese pharmaceutical firms and their U.S. and Europe-based counterparts.

title: A peptide-guided strategy for kinase activation

summary: FDA announced it is soliciting input on efforts with respect to drug repurposing to help address unmet medical needs across a range of diseases and conditions.

summary: FDA issued an approval for Bizengri (zenocutuzumab-zbco), a drug that treats NRG1 fusion-positive cholangiocarcinoma, an ultra-rare, aggressive cancer that forms in the bile ducts.
